verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Prayers first do not interfere with dedication afterwards.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: This is what I have to complain of on the part of my accuser.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: But may you, most religious Augustus, live through the course of many years to come, and celebrate the dedication of the Church.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Surely the prayers which have been offered for your safety by all men, are no hindrance to this celebration.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Let these unlearned persons cease such misrepresentations, but let them learn from the example of the Fathers; and let them read the Scriptures.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Or rather let them learn of you, who are so well instructed in such histories, how that Joshua the son of Josedek the priest, and his brethren, and Zorobabel the wise, the son of Salathiel, and Ezra the priest and scribe of the law, when the temple was in course of building after the captivity, the feast of tabernacles being at hand (which was a great feast and time of assembly and prayer in Israel), gathered the people together with one accord in the great court within the first gate, which is toward the East, and prepared the altar to God, and there offered their gifts, and kept the feast.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: And so afterwards they brought hither their sacrifices, on the sabbaths and the new moons, and the people offered up their prayers.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: And yet the Scripture says expressly, that when these things were done, the temple of God was not yet built; but rather while they thus prayed, the building of the house was advancing.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: So that neither were their prayers deferred in expectation of the dedication, nor was the dedication prevented by the assemblies held for the sake of prayer.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: But the people thus continued to pray; and when the house was entirely finished, they celebrated the dedication, and brought their gifts for that purpose, and all kept the feast for the completion of the work.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: And thus also have the blessed Alexander, and the other Fathers done.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: They continued to assemble their people, and when they had completed the work they gave thanks unto the Lord, and celebrated the dedication.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: This also it befits you to do, O Prince, most careful in your inquiries.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: The place is ready, having been already sanctified by the prayers which have been offered in it, and requires only the presence of your Piety.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: This only is wanting to its perfect beauty.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Do you then supply this deficiency, and there make your prayers unto the Lord, for whom you have built this house.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: That you may do so is the prayer of all men.
